page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance across memory bandwidth and computational workloads. The instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 18702.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 68952.47, reflecting high efficiency in multi-threaded tasks. Memory bandwidth tests show strong results, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4711.73 GB/sec and large block uncached writes at 92.79 GB/sec. Database operations are highly performant, as demonstrated by a Passmark database score of 27825.62 and Redis SET operations processing 70.49 million operations per second. However, LLM inference on larger models is a notable tradeoff, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model dropping to a weak 27.64 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i-flex.16xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory or storage-optimized hardware. The combination of DDR5 memory and Intel AMX acceleration makes it highly capable for web serving, software compilation, database operations, and light machine learning inference. It is particularly well-suited for static web serving, which achieved a throughput of 68.72 GB/sec, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 68952.47, Redis SET operations reaching 70.49 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 68.72 GB/sec. While it excels in software compilation and database operations, performance drops significantly for larger LLM inference tasks. This instance offers a balanced, cost-efficient profile for enterprise web serving, database management, and general-purpose computational workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for high-throughput database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 8488C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Strong database performance with 70.49 million Redis SET operations per second
High-throughput static web serving at 68.72 GB/sec
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and databases
